evals.name: verbose-narrative-comments
evals.prompt: The comments in case1_client.py are way too wordy and a lot of them just restate the code line below them. Tighten them up to our house comment style — keep the real reasons, drop the play-by-play — without changing any behavior.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step what-comments removed; the 429/Polarion-rate-limit rationale kept as a tight line; executable code byte-identical (AST unchanged); total comment volume cut substantially.

assertions.text: Executable code is unchanged: the output's AST with docstrings normalized equals the input's (no statement added/removed/reordered, no literal changed).
Pure what-comments that merely restate the next line are gone (e.g. 'Increment the attempt counter by one', 'Send the actual POST request', 'Check whether the response status code').
The load-bearing why survives in compressed form: a comment still conveys the 429 / Polarion ~3-req-per-second rate-limit rationale for retrying.
Total comment character count is reduced by at least 40% versus the input.
No comment is a multi-sentence paragraph and none carry NOTE:/WARNING: banner prefixes.

evals.name: preserve-boundaries
evals.prompt: Apply our code-comment cleanup to case2_tool.py — make the developer comments denser. Be careful about what's off-limits in this codebase.
evals.expected_output: @mcp.tool docstring and the Field(description=...) string left byte-identical; # noqa: E501 and # type: ignore[name-defined] preserved in place; the _build_update_payload helper's verbose comment compressed; code unchanged.

assertions.text: The @mcp.tool-decorated update_work_item function's docstring is byte-for-byte identical to the input (not shortened, not reworded).
The Field(description=...) string passed to work_item_id is byte-for-byte identical to the input.
The functional pseudo-comments '# noqa: E501' and '# type: ignore[name-defined]' are still present, attached to the same statements.
The verbose multi-line comment inside _build_update_payload is compressed to a tighter form (fewer characters), and the trailing 'Now return the fully assembled payload' what-comment is removed.
Executable code is unchanged: output AST with docstrings normalized equals input AST.

evals.name: what-comments-and-todo
evals.prompt: case3_payload.py is littered with comments that just narrate the code, plus a sloppy lowercase todo. Clean the comments up the way we do — only keep what the code can't say — and fix the todo.
evals.expected_output: Trivial what-comments removed; the Polarion None-default rationale kept (compressed); the lowercase 'todo:' reformatted to Google style # TODO(...): concrete action; code unchanged.

assertions.text: Trivial what-comments are removed: 'create a new empty dictionary', 'iterate over each of the attribute items', 'skip it and move on', 'store the value', and 'return the final result dictionary' no longer appear.
The why is kept: a comment still explains that Polarion treats None as clear-to-default so None entries are dropped (may be compressed to one line).
The TODO is in Google style: matches the pattern '# TODO(' owner ')' or at least '# TODO:' with uppercase TODO and a concrete action, not the original lowercase 'todo: ... not sure'.
Executable code is unchanged: output AST with docstrings normalized equals input AST.
